About: It is about Operation Barbarossa, Germany's surprise attack on the Soviet Union during World War II.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Mark Simonitch.
It is associated with Ty Bomba.
It is associated with David W. Nicholas.
It is associated with Matt Irsik.
It is associated with Don Johnson.
It is associated with Compass Games.
ConsistsOf: It consists of two large maps and 970 counters.
It consists of two 22" x 34" maps and over 2,000 counters in its deluxe edition.
HasA: It has a relatively simple "I Go, You Go" system of alternating turns.
It has no zones of control.
It has a Mobile Assault combat system.
It has Victory Point totals for Soviet cities.
It has a 50% chance of winning immediately if Moscow is captured.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being a magazine pull-out game.
It is characterized by having about a thousand counters.
It is characterized by its "I Go, You Go" system of alternating turns.
It is characterized by the Mobile Assault combat system.
It is characterized by "untried" Soviet units in its second edition.
It is characterized by Victory Point totals for Soviet cities.
It is characterized by its tight rules.
It is characterized by its exemplary rulebook.
It is characterized by its fun gameplay.
It is characterized by its straightforward nature.
IsA: It is a two-player board wargame.
It is a magazine pull-out game.
It is a boxed set in its deluxe edition.
KnownFor: It is known for simulating Operation Barbarossa.
It is known for being a "monster" game with many counters.
It is known for its tight rules and exemplary rulebook.
It is known for its fun gameplay.
It is known for its straightforward nature and fast gameplay for its size.
PublishedAs: It was published as a pull-out game in Issue 27 of Command (March–April 1994).
It was published as a pull-out game in Issue 34 of Command (September 1995).
It was published as a boxed set titled Proud Monster Deluxe in 2012.
PublishedBy: It was published by Compass Games.
PublishedIn: It was published in Issue 27 of Command (March–April 1994).
It was published in Issue 34 of Command (September 1995).
UsedFor: It is used for simulating Operation Barbarossa.
